questions.question_text: Who is responsible for maintaining transaction records in a Tennessee brokerage?
distractors: Each individual affiliate broker
The firm's office manager
TREC's records department
questions.explanation: The principal broker is responsible for maintaining all transaction records for the firm, including listing agreements, contracts, closing statements, and correspondence.

questions.question_text: Which documents must a Tennessee principal broker maintain as part of transaction records?
questions.correct_answer: Listings, contracts, closings, and trust records
distractors: Only the final closing statement documents
Only the signed purchase contracts received
Only the trust account bank statement records
questions.explanation: The broker must maintain comprehensive records including listing agreements, purchase contracts, closing statements, trust account records, correspondence, and offers.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker completes a transaction but keeps all the paperwork in their home office. Is this acceptable?
questions.correct_answer: No, the principal broker must maintain records
distractors: Yes, if the affiliate keeps organized files
Yes, for the first year after closing
Yes, if the broker has digital copies
questions.explanation: Transaction records must be maintained by the principal broker, not individual affiliates. The broker must have access to and control over all transaction documentation.

questions.question_text: A principal broker's record keeping system must ensure records are maintained in what manner?
distractors: Alphabetical by client last name only
In a fireproof safe at all times
On TREC-provided standardized forms
questions.explanation: Transaction documents must be maintained in an organized and accessible manner so they can be reviewed during audits or investigations by TREC.

questions.question_text: A broker receives written offers, counteroffers, and a listing modification. Which must be retained?
questions.correct_answer: All of them are required records
distractors: Only the final accepted offer
Only the listing and final contract
Only documents signed by both parties
questions.explanation: The principal broker must retain all transaction-related documents, including offers, counteroffers, amendments, and correspondence — not just the final executed contract.

questions.question_text: How long must a Tennessee brokerage keep transaction records after closing?
distractors: 1 year
5 years
7 years
questions.explanation: Records must be kept for 3 years from the date of closing, or from the termination of the listing if no closing occurs.

questions.question_text: A listing expires without a sale in June 2025. Until when must the broker retain the listing records?
distractors: June 2026
June 2030
December 2025
questions.explanation: The 3-year retention period runs from the termination of the listing when no closing occurs. A listing that expired in June 2025 must have records retained until June 2028.

questions.question_text: TREC investigators arrive at a brokerage to review transaction files. Must the broker provide access?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, TREC may access records for audits
distractors: Only with a signed court order first
Only if a complaint was already filed
Only during pre-scheduled annual reviews
questions.explanation: TREC can access records for audit and investigation purposes. Brokers must cooperate with TREC's request to review transaction records and trust account documentation.

questions.question_text: A broker destroys transaction records 2 years after closing to save storage space. What is the consequence?
distractors: No violation if digital copies exist
Acceptable if the firm has moved offices
No violation after the first year
questions.explanation: Destroying records before the 3-year retention period expires is a violation of TREC rules, regardless of the reason. The broker must maintain records for the full period.

questions.question_text: The 3-year record retention period begins on which date?
questions.correct_answer: The date of closing or listing termination
distractors: The date the listing agreement was signed
The date the offer was accepted
January 1 of the following year
questions.explanation: The 3-year retention period runs from the date of closing. If no closing occurs, it runs from the date of listing termination or expiration.

questions.question_text: When must a licensee provide copies of signed documents to all parties?
distractors: Within 5 business days of signing
At the closing only
Upon written request by a party
questions.explanation: Licensees must provide copies of all signed documents to all parties at the time of signing. This is not a delayed obligation — it must happen immediately.

questions.question_text: A seller's agent receives an offer that seems unreasonably low. Must the agent present it to the seller?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, all offers must be presented promptly
distractors: No, clearly unreasonable offers can be rejected
Only if the offer is within 10% of asking
Only after discussing it with the buyer's agent
questions.explanation: All offers must be presented to the seller promptly, regardless of the agent's opinion of the offer. The seller has the right to see and respond to every offer received.

questions.question_text: After a counteroffer is made, what documentation requirement applies?
questions.correct_answer: Copies must be provided to all parties
distractors: Only the original offer needs documentation
Counteroffers only need verbal communication
Documentation is optional for counteroffers
questions.explanation: Counteroffers and amendments must be documented and copies provided to all parties. The same documentation standards apply to counteroffers as to original offers.

questions.question_text: An affiliate broker delays presenting an offer to the seller for three days because they expected a better offer. What violation occurred?
distractors: No violation if a better offer did arrive
Only a violation if the seller complains
Acceptable business judgment by the agent
questions.explanation: A licensee cannot withhold or delay presentation of any offer. All offers must be presented promptly to the seller, regardless of whether the agent expects better offers.

questions.question_text: A buyer signs a purchase agreement on Tuesday. By when must the buyer receive a copy?
questions.correct_answer: On Tuesday at the time of signing
distractors: By end of business Wednesday
Within 3 business days
At the next meeting with the agent
questions.explanation: Copies of all signed documents must be provided at the time of signing. The buyer should receive their copy of the purchase agreement on Tuesday when they sign it.
